name: wisp
description: Fast, lightweight Nostr relay with spider mode that syncs notes from people you follow.
readme: # wisp - a lightweight nostr relay A fast, lightweight [nostr](https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nostr) relay written in Zig. ## Why Wisp? - **Fast**: ~9x higher throughput than strfry, sub-millisecond p99 latency - **Small**: Single binary, ~11MB RAM at idle - **Simple**: One command to run your personal relay with your feed - **Spider Mode**: Automatically syncs events from people you follow ## Quickstart ### Docker ```sh docker run -d -p 7777:7777 -v wisp-data:/data ghcr.io/privkeyio/wisp --spider-admin npub1yourkey... ``` ### Build from source Download the [latest release](https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/releases) or build from source: ```sh # 1. Install dependencies (requires Zig 0.16.0) sudo apt install -y liblmdb-dev libsecp256k1-dev libssl-dev # 2. Build git clone https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp && cd wisp zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast # 3. Run (replace with your npub) ./zig-out/bin/wisp --spider-admin npub1yourkey... ``` That's it. Wisp will fetch your follow list, sync notes from popular relays, and serve them at `ws://localhost:7777`. Add this relay URL to your Nostr client. For more options, see `wisp.toml.example`. ## Features * NIPs: 1, 2, 9, 11, 13, 16, 33, 40, 42, 45, 50, 65, 70, 77, 86 * LMDB storage (no external database) * Spider mode for syncing events from external relays * Import/export to JSONL * Prometheus metrics at `GET /metrics` ## Monitoring Operational metrics are exposed in Prometheus format at `GET /metrics` on the relay's port (connections, events stored/rejected/broadcast, REQ count, rate limiting). The endpoint honors the relay's IP allowlist/blocklist, so restrict it there or at your reverse proxy/firewall if you don't want it public. ## Import/Export ```sh ./zig-out/bin/wisp export > backup.jsonl ./zig-out/bin/wisp import < backup.jsonl ``` ## Configuration Copy `wisp.toml.example` to `wisp.toml` and customize, or use environment variables with the `WISP_` prefix: ```sh WISP_PORT=8080 ./zig-out/bin/wisp ``` Environment variables override the config file. See the **[Configuration reference](docs/configuration.md)** for every setting, its environment variable, default, and meaning. ## Deploy (wss://) To make your relay publicly accessible with TLS, run wisp with Caddy: ```sh # Run wisp docker run -d --restart always -p 7777:7777 -v wisp-data:/data \ ghcr.io/privkeyio/wisp --spider-admin npub1yourkey... # Install Caddy for automatic TLS sudo apt install -y caddy ``` Create `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile`: ``` relay.yourdomain.com { reverse_proxy localhost:7777 } ``` ```sh sudo systemctl restart caddy ``` Your relay is now live at `wss://relay.yourdomain.com`. ## Benchmarks Peak write throughput, all relays at 100% delivery: | Relay | Events/sec | p99 Latency | Memory (RSS) | |-------|-----------:|------------:|-------------:| | **Wisp** | **25,600** | **0.28 ms** | 11 MB | | nostr-rs-relay | 5,400 | 5.9 ms | 20 MB | | strfry | 2,800 | 2.9 ms | 3 MB | *[nostr-bench](https://github.com/privkeyio/nostr-bench), 5,000 events x 4 workers at peak rate (`--rate 0`), native release builds on a 16-core Linux host, with the event-rate limit raised. `RssAnon` sampled during the run. These are peak figures with Wisp in `sync = none` (non-durable); Wisp's default is `sync = meta` (durable, never corrupts), which trades raw throughput for crash safety. strfry and nostr-rs-relay write durably by default, so a fair durable comparison runs Wisp in `meta`/`full` (see [benchmarks](docs/benchmarks.md)). Wisp leads on throughput (~9x strfry, ~5x nostr-rs-relay) and p99 latency; strfry stays smallest in memory.* ## Contributing See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on reporting issues and submitting pull requests. ## Changelog See [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for a history of changes to this project. ## License MIT

releases.name: v0.5.1
releases.body:
Patch release.

### Fixed
- Spider no longer escalates productive but short-lived upstream connections into multi-hour blackouts (the cause of feeds freezing). A connection that synced events resets the reconnect backoff regardless of duration; backoff/blackout caps lowered to 5 min / 30 min. (#99)
- Re-pinned httpz to the simplified reorder fix. (#98)

## What's Changed
* Re-pin httpz to the simplified websocket lost-read fix by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/98
* Fix spider backoff escalating productive syncs into long blackouts by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/99
* Release v0.5.1 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/102


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/compare/v0.5.0...v0.5.1

releases.name: v0.5.0
releases.body:
### Changed
- Storage `sync` now defaults to `meta` (durable, never corrupts) instead of `none`. Operators upgrading get crash-safe writes by default; set `sync = none` for maximum throughput on disposable data.
- Updated libnostr-z to v0.3.6.

### Fixed
- Lost WebSocket read events under load that could leak connections (CLOSE_WAIT) and hang clients, e.g. an authed publish whose NIP-42 challenge arrived in the same packet as the upgrade response (http.zig PR #212 follow-up plus libnostr-z v0.3.6 websocket fixes).
- No longer send `CLOSED` in reply to a client `CLOSE` (NIP-01).
- Config file argument parsing and inline comment handling.
- Spider connection handling.

## What's Changed
* Add configuration reference and refresh benchmarks by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/88
* Add configurable LMDB write durability (storage sync mode) by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/91
* Add group-commit writer for durable sync modes by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/93
* Default storage sync to meta for crash safety by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/95
* Release v0.5.0 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/96


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/compare/v0.4.2...v0.5.0

releases.name: v0.4.2
releases.body:
**Relay fixes**
- Kind/pubkey index queries now honor `since`/`until`; a time-bounded query no longer returns events outside the window.
- An empty kind index no longer returns an event of a different kind via the first-entry path (now prefix-checked, scoped to the kind/pubkey indexes).

**Tooling**
- Integration tests and CI now use noz (privkeyio/noz v0.2.0) instead of nak, removing the Go-toolchain dependency. The two relay fixes above were surfaced by noz, which trusts the relay rather than re-filtering client-side.

NIP-11 advertised version synced to 0.4.2.

## What's Changed
* Swap test client from nak to noz by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/86


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/compare/v0.4.1...v0.4.2

releases.name: v0.4.1
releases.body:
### Fixed
- No longer send `CLOSED` in reply to a client `CLOSE` (NIP-01 compliance).

### Changed
- Updated libnostr-z to v0.3.3 (WebSocket frame-loss and TCP_NODELAY fixes).

## What's Changed
* Scale epoll workers to host CPU count by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/69
* Keep request-handler pool constant across epoll workers by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/70
* Add Prometheus metrics endpoint (#50) by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/72
* Add scanner unit tests, fuzz targets, and stress coverage by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/73
* Add connection matcher and NIP-86 dispatch tests by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/76
* Fix event rate limiting for limits over 255 by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/79
* Reduce relay memory footprint by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/80
* Security hardening: query rate limiting, IP matching, proxy headers, negentropy auth by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/82
* Don't send CLOSED in reply to client CLOSE (NIP-01) by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/84
* Bump Dockerfile Zig to 0.16.0 to match build by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/85


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/compare/v0.4.0...v0.4.1

releases.name: v0.4.0
releases.body:
Epoll worker-pool I/O — fixes per-connection memory growth under churn.

This release replaces wisp's thread-per-connection model with a **bounded epoll worker pool** (via http.zig + websocket.zig), resolving #64. Connections no longer create/destroy OS threads, so memory stays flat under connection churn.

## Highlights
- **#64 fixed**: churning 4000 connections, RssAnon now **plateaus (~1.1 MB, flat)** instead of growing linearly (the old model climbed 3.6 → 20 MB with no plateau). Threads stay bounded (no 2-per-connection).
- HTTP (NIP-11/NIP-86/CORS) and WebSocket upgrades served on one port by http.zig; WS connections run on websocket.zig's epoll worker pool.
- Unified, thread-safe write path: REQ streaming and broadcast both go through one `Connection.write`; the per-connection write-queue thread is gone.
- Concurrency-hardened: broadcasts snapshot targets under the registry lock and write after releasing it (a slow client can't stall others), with a write-guard refcount preventing use-after-free; send timeout + idle shutdown preserved.
- New dependency: http.zig (Zig 0.16), reusing the websocket.zig commit wisp already pinned.

## Compatibility
- Requires **Zig 0.16**. Behavior and supported NIPs are unchanged; validated against the full integration suite (protocol, NIP-42/45/50/70/77/86, negentropy, spider, concurrency).
- For Zig 0.15, use [`v0.2.2`](https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/releases/tag/v0.2.2).

releases.name: v0.3.0
releases.body:
Zig 0.16 support, with reliability and correctness fixes.

This release migrates wisp to **Zig 0.16** (stable) and hardens several paths. The relay architecture is unchanged (thread-per-connection); the epoll I/O rework is tracked separately.

## Highlights
- **Zig 0.16**: time, randomness, filesystem, networking, and sync moved to the new `std.Io` interface. Dependencies: libnostr-z v0.3.0, a 0.16-compatible websocket.zig.
- **SIGPIPE**: a client disconnecting mid-write no longer terminates the process (now an `EPIPE` handled by the write paths). The relay survives heavy concurrent-connection load.
- **Accept loop**: replaced the `SO_RCVTIMEO`-on-accept trick (which panics under 0.16) with `poll()` + accept-when-ready, preserving prompt shutdown.
- **Spider sync**: reconnects and re-subscribes when the follow list changes (adds/removals/replacements), fixing missed catch-up after the contact list is bootstrapped post-connect.
- **Per-IP connection limit**: atomic `tryAcquireConnection` closes a check-then-act race.
- **IPv6**: client IPs are normalized (brackets stripped) for ACLs and per-IP limits.
- **import/export**: I/O errors now propagate (non-zero exit) instead of being silently treated as completion.
- Minor leak guards in the subscription candidate paths.

## Compatibility
- Requires **Zig 0.16** to build. For Zig 0.15, use [`v0.2.2`](https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/releases/tag/v0.2.2).

Validated against the full integration suite (protocol, NIP-42/45/50/70/77/86, negentropy, spider, concurrency).

* Update benchmark results by @kwsantiago in https://github.com/privkeyio/wisp/pull/31

owner_bio: PrivKey is an enterprise cybersecurity firm specializing in Bitcoin, Nostr & Lightning Network infrastructure and institutional-grade digital asset security.
